R Notebook

library(brms)
library(tidyverse)
library(BayesFactor)
library(BayesianId)
data("penguins")
test_data = na.omit(penguins) %>% rename(y = body_mass, x1 = flipper_len, x2 = bill_len, f1 = island, f2 = sex)
test_data = test_data |> dplyr::mutate(y_bin = test_data$f2, y_cens = ifelse(y > 5000, 5000, y), censoring = (y > 5000))

levels(test_data$f1) |> print()
[1] "Biscoe"    "Dream"     "Torgersen"
levels(test_data$f2) |> print()
[1] "female" "male"  
nrow(test_data) |> print()
[1] 333
tic = Sys.time()
bar = breg(f2 ~ x1 + x2, 
           data = test_data,
           #family = "normal_linear",
           #family = "right_censored_linear",
           family = "bernoulli_logistic",
           chains = 4,
           iter = 10000,
           center = TRUE
           )
toc = Sys.time()
print(toc - tic)
Time difference of 35.43806 secs
summary(bar)
Call:
breg(formula = y ~ x1 + x2, data = test_data, family = "normal_linear", 
    center = TRUE, chains = 2, iter = 5000)

Sampling details and estimates:
Inference for Stan model: normal_linear_est.
2 chains, each with iter=5000; warmup=1250; thin=1; 
post-warmup draws per chain=3750, total post-warmup draws=7500.

              mean se_mean     sd     2.5%
Intercept 4206.752   0.261 21.541 4164.698
x1          48.744   0.028  2.062   44.646
x2           5.257   0.074  5.256   -5.088
             97.5% n_eff Rhat
Intercept 4249.470  6796    1
x1          52.705  5280    1
x2          15.587  5056    1

Samples were drawn using NUTS(diag_e) at Wed Mar  4 15:01:28 2026.
For each parameter, n_eff is a crude measure of effective sample size,
and Rhat is the potential scale reduction factor on split chains (at 
convergence, Rhat=1).
barfight = glm(f2 ~ x1 + x2, data = prepare_data(parse_formula(f2 ~ x1 + x2), test_data), family = "binomial")
barfight |> coef() |> round(3)
(Intercept)          x1          x2 
      0.020       0.008       0.124 
Barsoom = lapreg(f2 ~ x1 + x2, data = test_data, family = "bernoulli_logistic")
Barsoom$optim_fit$par |> round(3)
Intercept        x1        x2 
    0.020     0.008     0.123 
print(Barsoom)
lapreg model object
Call:
lapreg(formula = f2 ~ x1 + x2, data = test_data, family = "bernoulli_logistic")
summary(Barsoom)
Call:
lapreg(formula = f2 ~ x1 + x2, data = test_data, family = "bernoulli_logistic")

    0.020     0.008     0.123

      0.020       0.008       0.124

x2          0.00002 -0.00020  0.00085

x1              0.00000  0.00012 -0.00021

f2 ~ x1 + x2

plot(Barsoom)

plot(bar)

data("Loblolly")
Loblolly = as.data.frame(Loblolly)
Loblolly$Seed = factor(Loblolly$Seed)
Loblolly[1:15,]
foo = parse_formula(height ~ age + (age | Seed))
foo
foo$random
bar = paste(foo$lhs, "~", paste(foo$random$Seed, collapse = " + ")) |> formula()
bar
Xg_list = list()
for(i in 1:length(levels(Loblolly$Seed))){
  Xg_list[[i]] = model.matrix(bar, data = Loblolly |> subset(Seed == levels(Loblolly$Seed)[[i]]))
}
Xg = Matrix::bdiag(Xg_list)
Xg
glmm_matrix = function(formula, data){
  formula_info = parse_formula(formula)
  gfactor = names(formula_info$random)[1]
  gef_formula = paste(foo$lhs, "~", paste(formula_info$random[[gfactor]], collapse = " + ")) |> formula()
  
  Xg_list = list()
  for(i in 1:length(levels(data[,gfactor]))){
    data_subset = data[data[,gfactor] == levels(data[,gfactor])[[i]],]
    Xg_list[[i]] = model.matrix(gef_formula, data = data_subset)
  }
  Xg = bdiag(Xg_list)
  return(Xg)
}
glmm_matrix(height ~ age + (age | Seed), data = Loblolly)
